title: Representing a Bounding Box
concept: A bounding box represents an object's location using four coordinates, typically in (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) or (x, y, w, h) format.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What type of machine learning head predicts bounding box coordinates?
exercises.explain: Bounding box coordinates are continuous numbers, so they are predicted by a regression head.

cards.title: GPS for an Image
cards.body: If you want to tell a delivery driver where to drop a package, you give them a latitude and longitude. Similarly, to tell a computer where an object is in an image, we need a standard set of coordinates to draw a box.

cards.title: Bounding Box Formats
cards.body: A Bounding Box is represented by 4 numbers. Common formats are corner coordinates $(x_{min}, y_{min}, x_{max}, y_{max})$ or centroid coordinates $(x_c, y_c, w, h)$ where $(x_c, y_c)$ is the center and $(w, h)$ are width and height.

cards.title: Placing a Sticker
cards.body: Imagine placing a rectangular sticker on a notebook. You can describe its position by pointing to the top-left and bottom-right corners, or by pointing to its center point and stating how wide and tall the sticker is.

cards.title: Formatting a Box
cards.body: For a dog in a 500x500 image, its bounding box might be represented as [100, 150, 300, 400] using corners, or [200, 275, 200, 250] using center coordinates, width, and height.

cards.title: Not always pixel coordinates
cards.body: It's a myth that bounding boxes always use raw pixel values. In deep learning, we almost always normalize these numbers to be fractions between 0 and 1 so they work regardless of the image resolution.

cards.title: Two Coordinate Systems
cards.body: Comparing the two primary ways to represent a bounding box:

left.text:
Uses top-left and bottom-right:
[xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax]

right.text:
Uses center, width, height:
[x_center, y_center, w, h]

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the corner coordinate format for a bounding box?
exercises.explain: The corner format represents the box using the minimum and maximum coordinates of the x and y axes: (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax).

options.text: The top-left corner coordinates and the bottom-right corner coordinates
The center point, width, and height of the box
The class label and confidence score
The rotation angle and scale factor

exercises.prompt: True or False: Center format (x, y, w, h) uses the top-left corner as the coordinate origin (x, y).
exercises.explain: False. In center format, (x, y) represents the exact center point of the bounding box, not the top-left corner.
The center-width-height format is commonly abbreviated as xywh (x_center, y_center, width, height).
exercises.sentence: The bounding box format that uses center coordinate, width, and height is often written as ___.

exercises.prompt: Which list of numbers represents a bounding box in corner format (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)?
exercises.explain: Representing a box with four boundary coordinates (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) corresponds to the corner format.

exercises.prompt: You are converting a dataset labeled in corner format [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] to center format [x_center, y_center, w, h]. If a box is defined as [10, 20, 50, 80], what is its width (w) and height (h)?
exercises.explain: Exactly! Width is calculated as xmax - xmin (50 - 10 = 40) and height is ymax - ymin (80 - 20 = 60). Next: let's learn why we normalize these coordinates!
